instruction1:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Main: added index_basket = ['NSE:RELIANCE', 'NSE:TCS'] and time_window = 30 variables (example values).",
    "Main: changed start_date calculation from `datetime.now().date() - pd.DateOffset(days=time_window)` to `(datetime.now() - timedelta(days=time_window)).date()` (uses timedelta instead of pd.DateOffset).",
    "Main: added all_price_data = [] and a for-loop to iterate over index_basket, calling fetch_price_data and calculate_daily_returns for each index (replaced the single fetch of index_basket[0]).",
    "Main: restructured flow to fetch and process multiple indices (loop), though the loop body is incomplete and ends with a stray `price_data` line.",
    "No changes to the individual utility functions (fetch_price_data, calculate_daily_returns, calculate_average_return, calculate_standard_deviation, calculate_momentum, rank_stocks, export_to_excel) — they remain functionally the same as in code1."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "Test case 1: Fetch price data for a specific index and date range": true,
    "Test case 2: Calculate daily returns on a sample price data DataFrame": true,
    "Test case 3: Calculate average return from daily returns": true,
    "Test case 4: Calculate standard deviation of daily returns": true,
    "Test case 5: Calculate momentum on sample price data": true
  },
  "not_implemented": []
}
instruction2:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Replaced psycopg2 usage with SQLAlchemy: removed psycopg2 import and added sqlalchemy.create_engine import.",
    "create_connection changed from returning a psycopg2 connection to returning a SQLAlchemy engine (create_engine with postgresql URL).",
    "ingest_csv_data changed from iterating rows and executing cursor.execute INSERT statements to using pandas.DataFrame.to_sql with the SQLAlchemy engine (bulk append).",
    "Removed manual cursor, commit, and connection.close() boilerplate in ingest_csv_data; replaced with df.to_sql which handles insertions.",
    "analyze_data updated to obtain data using the SQLAlchemy engine (pd.read_sql_query(engine)) and no longer calls conn.close(); it now returns efficiency_issues directly.",
    "Minor imports and dependencies adjusted: removed psycopg2 and added SQLAlchemy import; other modules (pandas, schedule, time, agenticai) retained.",
    "Kept schedule_csv_drop, generate_report, and main structure, but their internals now operate against the SQLAlchemy engine rather than a psycopg2 connection."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "Test case 1 (analyze_data returns list of efficiency issues without error)": false,
    "Test case 2 (ingest_csv_data runs without error)": false,
    "Test case 3 (create_connection establishes connection/non-None object)": true,
    "Test case 4 (generate_report creates CSV without errors)": true,
    "Test case 5 (schedule_csv_drop runs without error)": true
  },
  "not_implemented": [
    "analyze_data still references undefined identifiers (threshold_value and calculate_savings). Because these are not defined in code2, calling analyze_data will raise a NameError and will not reliably return a list of issues as the test expects.",
    "ingest_csv_data still uses a hard-coded CSV path ('path/to/csv_file.csv') and has no file existence check, error handling, or mocking for tests. The test's expectation that ingest_csv_data can be called without errors (in a test environment) is not addressed."
  ]
}

instruction25:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Added Flask app creation and configuration (app = Flask(__name__), app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'], app.config['SECRET_KEY']).",
    "Added imports for Flask, request, jsonify, Flask SQLAlchemy and Flask-Login (from flask import ..., from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy, from flask_login import ...).",
    "Initialized SQLAlchemy (db = SQLAlchemy(app)) and LoginManager (login_manager = LoginManager(app)).",
    "Defined a concrete User model class (class User(db.Model, UserMixin)) with fields id, username (unique), password, role.",
    "Kept the same /register, /login, /logout route handlers, but they now operate against the defined User model and database session.",
    "Added db.create_all() and an if __name__ == '__main__' entrypoint calling app.run(debug=True) to make the app runnable.",
    "Marked username column as unique=True (database-level uniqueness constraint)."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "Test user registration": true,
    "Test user login with valid credentials": true,
    "Test user login with invalid credentials": true,
    "Test user logout": true,
    "Test user registration with existing username": false
  },
  "not_implemented": [
    "Graceful handling of registration when username already exists: code2 adds a unique constraint at the DB level but does not check for an existing username or catch IntegrityError to return a friendly error response."
  ]
}
instruction28:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Added missing imports: Flask, jsonify, request, requests, threading, time, websocket.",
    "Created Flask app instance: app = Flask(__name__).",
    "Initialized global state variables: live_prices = {} and order_status = 'Pending'.",
    "Wrapped startup logic in an if __name__ == '__main__' block.",
    "Started the fetch_live_prices background updater by launching it in a separate thread: threading.Thread(target=fetch_live_prices).start().",
    "Kept the existing Flask route handlers (GET /live-prices, GET /public-ip, POST /trade, GET /order-status) but integrated them into the initialized Flask app.",
    "Added startup sequence to call start_websocket() and app.run(debug=True) from the main block.",
    "Reordered and packaged the script to be runnable as a standalone application."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "Expose a callable that returns live prices for direct invocation by tests (i.e., a candidate() function that returns data when called multiple times)": false,
    "Add missing imports and initialize Flask app so the application can run": true,
    "Initialize shared globals (live_prices and order_status) so endpoints have defined state": true,
    "Start the live price updater in the background so live_prices get updated over time": true,
    "Use an if __name__ == '__main__' guard to start background tasks and run the app": true,
    "Start the websocket without blocking the main thread (i.e., run start_websocket in its own thread so app.run is reachable)": false
  },
  "not_implemented": [
    "Provide a direct callable (e.g., candidate() or a non-blocking function) that returns current live_prices for test harnesses that call candidate() repeatedly.",
    "Run the WebSocket client in a non-blocking manner (start_websocket is called directly in main and calls ws.run_forever(), which will block and prevent app.run from executing)."
  ]
}
instruction37:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Added Flask application setup (Flask app creation and configuration).",
    "Added imports for Flask, request, jsonify, session, werkzeug security helpers, and Flask SQLAlchemy.",
    "Configured SQLAlchemy with a SQLite database URI ('sqlite:///hiregenz.db').",
    "Added app.config['SECRET_KEY'] for session support.",
    "Initialized SQLAlchemy (db = SQLAlchemy(app)).",
    "Introduced a User model with id, username (unique=True), and password fields.",
    "Converted register() into a routed endpoint: @app.route('/register', methods=['POST']).",
    "Kept and exposed the login() endpoint as @app.route('/login', methods=['POST']) (same logic but now within full app context).",
    "Added db.create_all() in the main block to create database tables.",
    "Added app.run(debug=True) in the main block to run the application.",
    "Kept password hashing (generate_password_hash) and password verification (check_password_hash) logic as in code1."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "register_route": true,
    "database_and_model_setup": true,
    "unique_username_constraint_in_database": true,
    "password_hashing": true,
    "login_success_and_failure_flow": true,
    "session_management_with_secret_key": true,
    "graceful_handling_of_duplicate_registration": false,
    "input_validation_for_missing_fields": false,
    "explicit_error_handling_for_db_integrity_errors": false
  },
  "not_implemented": [
    "Graceful handling of duplicate username registration (e.g., check existing user and return a friendly 4xx response instead of relying solely on DB constraint).",
    "Input validation for required fields (no checks for presence/format of 'username' and 'password').",
    "Catching and returning meaningful errors for database integrity exceptions (e.g., IntegrityError) to avoid 500 responses on duplicates.",
    "Any additional security/hardening around password policy, rate limiting, or login brute-force protection (not requested explicitly in test but commonly expected)."
  ]
}
instruction41:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Added a 10-second timeout to requests.get calls (requests.get(..., timeout=10)).",
    "Called response.raise_for_status() to raise for non-2xx HTTP responses instead of manually checking status_code.",
    "Changed error handling in fetch_stock_data to catch requests.exceptions.RequestException, print a descriptive error message, and return None on request-related failures (instead of raising an Exception on non-200 responses).",
    "Added a truthiness check in analyze_stock_data (if stock_data:) to avoid iterating over None or missing data.",
    "Wrapped the SMTP send block in send_email_alert with a try/except to catch and print exceptions during email sending instead of letting them propagate.",
    "Removed the per-country try/except in main (originally catching all exceptions per country and printing them) — main now relies on fetch_stock_data returning None for request errors and does not explicitly catch exceptions inside the country loop."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "Test case 1 (Spain returns JSON data)": true,
    "Test case 2 (USA returns JSON data)": true,
    "Test case 3 (Australia returns JSON data)": true,
    "Test case 4 (Invalid country should raise an Exception)": true,
    "Test case 5 (Country with no data should raise Exception or return empty list)": true
  },
  "not_implemented": []
}
instruction44:
{
  "modifications": [
    "Replaced the single call to schedule.run_pending() with an infinite loop that repeatedly calls schedule.run_pending() and sleeps for 1 second (while True: schedule.run_pending(); time.sleep(1)).",
    "Because of the new infinite loop placed before app.run(), the Flask server start (app.run(...)) is now unreachable / will never be executed.",
    "Removed an unused import: timedelta (changed from 'from datetime import datetime, timedelta' to 'from datetime import datetime').",
    "No other changes to API URL construction, request params, or the order counting logic."
  ],
  "test_implemented": {
    "initial_order_count_should_be_accessible": false,
    "fetch_daily_order_count_callable_and_updates_count": true,
    "reset_order_count_callable_and_resets_to_zero": true,
    "subsequent_fetch_updates_count": true,
    "midnight_reset_is_scheduled": true,
    "scheduler_run_loop_provided": true,
    "flask_server_runs_concurrently_with_scheduler": false,
    "get_order_count_returns_plain_dict_like_test_expected": false
  },
  "not_implemented": [
    "The test expects calling candidate() to return a plain Python dict like {'order_count': 0}. get_order_count() returns a Flask Response (jsonify), so the exact test expectation (plain dict) is not met.",
    "The test appears to expect a single callable object 'candidate' that exposes fetch_daily_order_count and reset_order_count as attributes/methods. Code2 keeps these as module-level functions and does not provide such a single callable object with those attached methods.",
    "Flask server does not run concurrently with the scheduler in code2 because the infinite while True loop blocks before app.run(), so the server never starts."
  ]
}
